#define MAVLINK_ENABLED_COMMON
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_HL_FAILURE_FLAG
 Flags to report failure cases over the high latency telemetry.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GOTO
 Actions that may be specified in MAV_CMD_OVERRIDE_GOTO to override mission execution.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MODE
 Predefined OR-combined MAV_MODE_FLAG values. These can simplify using the flags when setting modes. Note that manual input is enabled in all modes as a safety override.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR
 These encode the sensors whose status is sent as part of the SYS_STATUS message.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED
 These encode the sensors whose status is sent as part of the SYS_STATUS message in the extended fields.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FRAME
 Coordinate frames used by MAVLink. Not all frames are supported by all commands, messages, or vehicles.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_BREACH
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_MITIGATE
 Actions being taken to mitigate/prevent fence breach.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_TYPE
 Fence types to enable or disable when using MAV_CMD_DO_FENCE_ENABLE. Note that at least one of these flags must be set in MAV_CMD_DO_FENCE_ENABLE.param2. If none are set, the flight stack will ignore the field and enable/disable its default set of fences (usually all of them).
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MOUNT_MODE
 Enumeration of possible mount operation modes. This message is used by obsolete/deprecated gimbal messages.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS
 Gimbal device (low level) capability flags (bitmap).
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS
 Gimbal manager high level capability flags (bitmap). The flags are identical to the GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS. However, the gimbal manager does not need to copy the flags from the gimbal but can also enhance the capabilities and thus add flags.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS
 Flags for gimbal device (lower level) operation.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS
 Flags for high level gimbal manager operation The first 16 bits are identical to the GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS
 Gimbal device (low level) error flags (bitmap, 0 means no error)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GRIPPER_ACTIONS
 Gripper actions.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_WINCH_ACTIONS
 Winch actions.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH
 Generalized UAVCAN node health.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_UAVCAN_NODE_MODE
 Generalized UAVCAN node mode.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE
 Indicates the ESC connection type.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS
 Flags to report ESC failures.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_STATUS
 Flags to indicate the status of camera storage.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_TYPE
 Flags to indicate the type of storage.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG
 Flags to indicate usage for a particular storage (see STORAGE_INFORMATION.storage_usage and MAV_CMD_SET_STORAGE_USAGE).
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR
 Yaw behaviour during orbit flight.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE
 Possible responses from a WIFI_CONFIG_AP message.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE
 Possible responses from a CELLULAR_CONFIG message.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE
 WiFi Mode.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_COMP_METADATA_TYPE
 Supported component metadata types. These are used in the "general" metadata file returned by COMPONENT_METADATA to provide information about supported metadata types. The types are not used directly in MAVLink messages.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION
 Actuator configuration, used to change a setting on an actuator. Component information metadata can be used to know which outputs support which commands.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION
 Actuator output function. Values greater or equal to 1000 are autopilot-specific.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_AUTOTUNE_AXIS
 Axes that will be autotuned by MAV_CMD_DO_AUTOTUNE_ENABLE. Note that at least one flag must be set in MAV_CMD_DO_AUTOTUNE_ENABLE.param2: if none are set, the flight stack will tune its default set of axes.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION
 Actions for reading/writing parameters between persistent and volatile storage when using MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE. (Commonly parameters are loaded from persistent storage (flash/EEPROM) into volatile storage (RAM) on startup and written back when they are changed.)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION
 Actions for reading and writing plan information (mission, rally points, geofence) between persistent and volatile storage when using MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE. (Commonly missions are loaded from persistent storage (flash/EEPROM) into volatile storage (RAM) on startup and written back when they are changed.)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION
 Reboot/shutdown action for selected component in MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS
 Specifies the conditions under which the MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN command should be accepted.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER
 Action for the magnetometer (param2) of MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER
 Action for the accelerometer (param5) of MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_CMD
 Commands to be executed by the MAV. They can be executed on user request, or as part of a mission script. If the action is used in a mission, the parameter mapping to the waypoint/mission message is as follows: Param 1, Param 2, Param 3, Param 4, X: Param 5, Y:Param 6, Z:Param 7. This command list is similar what ARINC 424 is for commercial aircraft: A data format how to interpret waypoint/mission data. NaN and INT32_MAX may be used in float/integer params (respectively) to indicate optional/default values (e.g. to use the component's current yaw or latitude rather than a specific value). See https://mavlink.io/en/guide/xml_schema.html#MAV_CMD for information about the structure of the MAV_CMD entries.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DATA_STREAM
 A data stream is not a fixed set of messages, but rather a recommendation to the autopilot software. Individual autopilots may or may not obey the recommended messages.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ROI
 The ROI (region of interest) for the vehicle. This can be be used by the vehicle for camera/vehicle attitude alignment (see MAV_CMD_NAV_ROI).
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_TYPE
 Specifies the datatype of a MAVLink parameter.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_ERROR
 Parameter protocol error types (see PARAM_ERROR).
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE
 Specifies the datatype of a MAVLink extended parameter.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_RESULT
 Result from a MAVLink command (MAV_CMD)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_RESULT
 Result of mission operation (in a MISSION_ACK message).
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SEVERITY
 Indicates the severity level, generally used for status messages to indicate their relative urgency. Based on RFC-5424 using expanded definitions at: http://www.kiwisyslog.com/kb/info:-syslog-message-levels/.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_POWER_STATUS
 Power supply status flags (bitmask)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV
 SERIAL_CONTROL device types.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG
 SERIAL_CONTROL flags (bitmask)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR
 Enumeration of distance sensor types.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION
 Enumeration of sensor orientation, according to its rotations.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_TYPE
 Type of mission items being requested/sent in mission protocol.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE
 Enumeration of estimator types.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_TYPE
 Enumeration of battery types.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION
 Enumeration of battery functions.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE
 Enumeration for battery charge states.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_MODE
 Battery mode. Note, the normal operation mode (i.e. when flying) should be reported as MAV_BATTERY_MODE_UNKNOWN to allow message trimming in normal flight.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FAULT
 Smart battery supply status/fault flags (bitmask) for health indication. The battery must also report either MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_FAILED or MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_UNHEALTHY if any of these are set.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FUEL_TYPE
 Fuel types for use in FUEL_TYPE. Fuel types specify the units for the maximum, available and consumed fuel, and for the flow rates.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG
 Flags to report status/failure cases for a power generator (used in GENERATOR_STATUS). Note that FAULTS are conditions that cause the generator to fail. Warnings are conditions that require attention before the next use (they indicate the system is not operating properly).
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_VTOL_STATE
 Enumeration of VTOL states.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_LANDED_STATE
 Enumeration of landed detector states.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ADSB_ALTITUDE_TYPE
 Enumeration of the ADSB altimeter types.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ADSB_EMITTER_TYPE
 ADSB classification for the type of vehicle emitting the transponder signal.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ADSB_FLAGS
 These flags indicate status such as data validity of each data source. Set = data valid.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DO_REPOSITION_FLAGS
 Bitmap of options for the MAV_CMD_DO_REPOSITION.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_SPEED_TYPE
 Speed setpoint types used in MAV_CMD_DO_CHANGE_SPEED.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ESTIMATOR_STATUS_FLAGS
 Flags in ESTIMATOR_STATUS message.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MOTOR_TEST_ORDER
 Sequence that motors are tested when using MAV_CMD_DO_MOTOR_TEST.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MOTOR_TEST_THROTTLE_TYPE
 Defines how throttle value is represented in MAV_CMD_DO_MOTOR_TEST.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GPS_INPUT_IGNORE_FLAGS
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_COLLISION_ACTION
 Possible actions an aircraft can take to avoid a collision.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_COLLISION_THREAT_LEVEL
 Aircraft-rated danger from this threat.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_COLLISION_SRC
 Source of information about this collision.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GPS_FIX_TYPE
 Type of GPS fix.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_RTK_BASELINE_COORDINATE_SYSTEM
 RTK GPS baseline coordinate system, used for RTK corrections.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_LANDING_TARGET_TYPE
 Type of landing target.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_VTOL_TRANSITION_HEADING
 Direction of VTOL transition.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_CAP_FLAGS
 Camera capability flags (Bitmap)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_VIDEO_STREAM_STATUS_FLAGS
 Stream status flags (Bitmap)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_VIDEO_STREAM_TYPE
 Video stream types.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_VIDEO_STREAM_ENCODING
 Video stream encodings.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_TRACKING_STATUS_FLAGS
 Camera tracking status flags.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_TRACKING_MODE
 Camera tracking modes.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_TRACKING_TARGET_DATA
 Camera tracking target data (shows where tracked target is within image)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_ZOOM_TYPE
 Zoom types for MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_ZOOM.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_SET_FOCUS_TYPE
 Focus types for MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_FOCUS.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_SOURCE
 Camera sources for MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_SOURCE.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_PARAM_ACK
 Result from PARAM_EXT_SET message.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_MODE
 Camera Modes.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ARM_AUTH_DENIED_REASON
 Reasons for denying an authorization request made with MAV_CMD_ARM_AUTHORIZATION_REQUEST. If the COMMAND_ACK result is MAV_RESULT_DENIED, this is used to set the reason in the result_param2 field.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_RC_TYPE
 RC type. Used in MAV_CMD_START_RX_PAIR.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_RC_SUB_TYPE
 RC sub-type of types defined in RC_TYPE. Used in MAV_CMD_START_RX_PAIR. Ignored if value does not correspond to the set RC_TYPE.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ENGINE_CONTROL_OPTIONS
 Engine control options.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_POSITION_TARGET_TYPEMASK
 Bitmap to indicate which dimensions should be ignored by the vehicle: a value of 0b0000000000000000 or 0b0000001000000000 indicates that none of the setpoint dimensions should be ignored. If bit 9 is set the floats afx afy afz should be interpreted as force instead of acceleration.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ATTITUDE_TARGET_TYPEMASK
 Bitmap to indicate which dimensions should be ignored by the vehicle: a value of 0b00000000 indicates that none of the setpoint dimensions should be ignored.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_UTM_FLIGHT_STATE
 Airborne status of UAS.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_UTM_DATA_AVAIL_FLAGS
 Flags for the global position report.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_STATUS_FLAG
 These flags encode the cellular network status.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_NETWORK_FAILED_REASON
 These flags are used to diagnose the failure state of CELLULAR_STATUS.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_NETWORK_RADIO_TYPE
 Cellular network radio type.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_PRECISION_LAND_MODE
 Precision land modes (used in MAV_CMD_NAV_LAND).
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_PARACHUTE_ACTION
 Parachute actions. Trigger release and enable/disable auto-release.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_TUNNEL_PAYLOAD_TYPE
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_ID_TYPE
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_UA_TYPE
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_STATUS
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_HEIGHT_REF
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_HOR_ACC
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_VER_ACC
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_SPEED_ACC
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_TIME_ACC
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_AUTH_TYPE
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_DESC_TYPE
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_OPERATOR_LOCATION_TYPE
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_CLASSIFICATION_TYPE
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_CATEGORY_EU
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_CLASS_EU
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_OPERATOR_ID_TYPE
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_ARM_STATUS
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_TUNE_FORMAT
 Tune formats (used for vehicle buzzer/tone generation).
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_AIS_TYPE
 Type of AIS vessel, enum duplicated from AIS standard, https://gpsd.gitlab.io/gpsd/AIVDM.html.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_AIS_NAV_STATUS
 Navigational status of AIS vessel, enum duplicated from AIS standard, https://gpsd.gitlab.io/gpsd/AIVDM.html.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_AIS_FLAGS
 These flags are used in the AIS_VESSEL.fields bitmask to indicate validity of data in the other message fields. When set, the data is valid.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_FAILURE_UNIT
 List of possible units where failures can be injected.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_FAILURE_TYPE
 List of possible failure type to inject.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_NAV_VTOL_LAND_OPTIONS
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_WINCH_STATUS_FLAG
 Winch status flags used in WINCH_STATUS.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAG_CAL_STATUS
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_EVENT_ERROR_REASON
 Reason for an event error response.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_EVENT_CURRENT_SEQUENCE_FLAGS
 Flags for CURRENT_EVENT_SEQUENCE.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_HIL_SENSOR_UPDATED_FLAGS
 Flags in the HIL_SENSOR message indicate which fields have updated since the last message.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_HIGHRES_IMU_UPDATED_FLAGS
 Flags in the HIGHRES_IMU message indicate which fields have updated since the last message.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_CAN_FILTER_OP
 

 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FTP_ERR
 MAV FTP error codes (may be used in FILE_TRANSFER_PROTOCOL). See https://mavlink.io/en/services/ftp.html.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FTP_OPCODE
 MAV FTP opcodes (may be used in FILE_TRANSFER_PROTOCOL). See https://mavlink.io/en/services/ftp.html.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MISSION_STATE
 States of the mission state machine. Note that these states are independent of whether the mission is in a mode that can execute mission items or not (is suspended). They may not all be relevant on all vehicles.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_SAFETY_SWITCH_STATE
 Possible safety switch states.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ILLUMINATOR_MODE
 Modes of illuminator.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_ILLUMINATOR_ERROR_FLAGS
 Illuminator module error flags (bitmap, 0 means no error)
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_STANDARD_MODE
 Standard modes with a well understood meaning across flight stacks and vehicle types. For example, most flight stack have the concept of a "return" or "RTL" mode that takes a vehicle to safety, even though the precise mechanics of this mode may differ. The modes supported by a flight stack can be queried using AVAILABLE_MODES and set using MAV_CMD_DO_SET_STANDARD_MODE. The current mode is streamed in CURRENT_MODE. See https://mavlink.io/en/services/standard_modes.html.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MODE_PROPERTY
 Mode properties.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS_FLAGS
 Flags used in HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS message.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_COMPUTER_STATUS_FLAGS
 Flags used to report computer status.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_AIRSPEED_SENSOR_FLAGS
 Airspeed sensor flags.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC
 Source for GLOBAL_POSITION measurement or estimate.
 
#define HAVE_ENUM_GLOBAL_POSITION_FLAGS
 Status flags for GLOBAL_POSITION.
 
#define MAVLINK_VERSION   3
 

Typedefs

typedef enum HL_FAILURE_FLAG HL_FAILURE_FLAG
 
typedef enum MAV_GOTO MAV_GOTO
 
typedef enum MAV_MODE MAV_MODE
 
typedef enum MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR
 
typedef enum MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED
 
typedef enum MAV_FRAME MAV_FRAME
 
typedef enum MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE
 
typedef enum FENCE_BREACH FENCE_BREACH
 
typedef enum FENCE_MITIGATE FENCE_MITIGATE
 
typedef enum FENCE_TYPE FENCE_TYPE
 
typedef enum MAV_MOUNT_MODE MAV_MOUNT_MODE
 
typedef enum GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS
 
typedef enum GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS

typedef enum GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS
 
typedef enum GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS
 
typedef enum GRIPPER_ACTIONS GRIPPER_ACTIONS
 
typedef enum WINCH_ACTIONS WINCH_ACTIONS

typedef enum UAVCAN_NODE_MODE UAVCAN_NODE_MODE
 
typedef enum ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE
 
typedef enum ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS

typedef enum STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG

typedef enum WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE

typedef enum ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION
 
typedef enum AUTOTUNE_AXIS AUTOTUNE_AXIS
 
typedef enum PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION
 
typedef enum PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION
 
typedef enum REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION
 
typedef enum REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS
 
typedef enum PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER
 
typedef enum PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER
 
typedef enum NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS

typedef enum MAV_DATA_STREAM MAV_DATA_STREAM
 
typedef enum MAV_ROI MAV_ROI
 
typedef enum MAV_PARAM_TYPE MAV_PARAM_TYPE
 
typedef enum MAV_PARAM_ERROR MAV_PARAM_ERROR
 
typedef enum MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE
 
typedef enum MAV_RESULT MAV_RESULT

typedef enum SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG
 
typedef enum MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR
 
typedef enum MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION
 
typedef enum MAV_MISSION_TYPE MAV_MISSION_TYPE
 
typedef enum MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE
 
typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_TYPE MAV_BATTERY_TYPE
 
typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION
 
typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE
 
typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_MODE MAV_BATTERY_MODE
 
typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_FAULT MAV_BATTERY_FAULT
 
typedef enum MAV_FUEL_TYPE MAV_FUEL_TYPE
 
typedef enum MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG
 
typedef enum MAV_VTOL_STATE MAV_VTOL_STATE
 
typedef enum MAV_LANDED_STATE MAV_LANDED_STATE
 
typedef enum ADSB_ALTITUDE_TYPE ADSB_ALTITUDE_TYPE
 
typedef enum ADSB_EMITTER_TYPE ADSB_EMITTER_TYPE
 
typedef enum ADSB_FLAGS ADSB_FLAGS
 
typedef enum MAV_DO_REPOSITION_FLAGS MAV_DO_REPOSITION_FLAGS
 
typedef enum SPEED_TYPE SPEED_TYPE
 
typedef enum ESTIMATOR_STATUS_FLAGS ESTIMATOR_STATUS_FLAGS
 
typedef enum MOTOR_TEST_ORDER MOTOR_TEST_ORDER
 
typedef enum MOTOR_TEST_THROTTLE_TYPE MOTOR_TEST_THROTTLE_TYPE
 
typedef enum GPS_INPUT_IGNORE_FLAGS GPS_INPUT_IGNORE_FLAGS
 
typedef enum MAV_COLLISION_ACTION MAV_COLLISION_ACTION
 
typedef enum MAV_COLLISION_THREAT_LEVEL MAV_COLLISION_THREAT_LEVEL
 
typedef enum MAV_COLLISION_SRC MAV_COLLISION_SRC
 
typedef enum GPS_FIX_TYPE GPS_FIX_TYPE
 
typedef enum RTK_BASELINE_COORDINATE_SYSTEM RTK_BASELINE_COORDINATE_SYSTEM
 
typedef enum LANDING_TARGET_TYPE LANDING_TARGET_TYPE
 
typedef enum VTOL_TRANSITION_HEADING VTOL_TRANSITION_HEADING
 
typedef enum CAMERA_CAP_FLAGS CAMERA_CAP_FLAGS
 
typedef enum VIDEO_STREAM_STATUS_FLAGS VIDEO_STREAM_STATUS_FLAGS
 
typedef enum VIDEO_STREAM_TYPE VIDEO_STREAM_TYPE
 
typedef enum VIDEO_STREAM_ENCODING VIDEO_STREAM_ENCODING
 
typedef enum CAMERA_TRACKING_STATUS_FLAGS CAMERA_TRACKING_STATUS_FLAGS
 
typedef enum CAMERA_TRACKING_MODE CAMERA_TRACKING_MODE
 
typedef enum CAMERA_TRACKING_TARGET_DATA CAMERA_TRACKING_TARGET_DATA
 
typedef enum CAMERA_ZOOM_TYPE CAMERA_ZOOM_TYPE
 
typedef enum SET_FOCUS_TYPE SET_FOCUS_TYPE
 
typedef enum CAMERA_SOURCE CAMERA_SOURCE

  MAV_FTP_OPCODE_OPENFILERO =4 , MAV_FTP_OPCODE_READFILE =5 , MAV_FTP_OPCODE_CREATEFILE =6 , MAV_FTP_OPCODE_WRITEFILE =7 ,
  MAV_FTP_OPCODE_REMOVEFILE =8 , MAV_FTP_OPCODE_CREATEDIRECTORY =9 , MAV_FTP_OPCODE_REMOVEDIRECTORY =10 , MAV_FTP_OPCODE_OPENFILEWO =11 ,

}
 
enum  MISSION_STATE {
  MISSION_STATE_UNKNOWN =0 , MISSION_STATE_NO_MISSION =1 , MISSION_STATE_NOT_STARTED =2 , MISSION_STATE_ACTIVE =3 ,
  MISSION_STATE_PAUSED =4 , MISSION_STATE_COMPLETE =5 , MISSION_STATE_ENUM_END =6
}
 
enum  SAFETY_SWITCH_STATE { SAFETY_SWITCH_STATE_SAFE =0 , SAFETY_SWITCH_STATE_DANGEROUS =1 , SAFETY_SWITCH_STATE_ENUM_END =2 }
 
enum  ILLUMINATOR_MODE { ILLUMINATOR_MODE_UNKNOWN =0 , ILLUMINATOR_MODE_INTERNAL_CONTROL =1 , ILLUMINATOR_MODE_EXTERNAL_SYNC =2 , ILLUMINATOR_MODE_ENUM_END =3 }
 
enum  ILLUMINATOR_ERROR_FLAGS { ILLUMINATOR_ERROR_FLAGS_THERMAL_THROTTLING =1 , ILLUMINATOR_ERROR_FLAGS_OVER_TEMPERATURE_SHUTDOWN =2 , ILLUMINATOR_ERROR_FLAGS_THERMISTOR_FAILURE =4 , ILLUMINATOR_ERROR_FLAGS_ENUM_END =5 }
 
enum  MAV_STANDARD_MODE {
  MAV_STANDARD_MODE_NON_STANDARD =0 , MAV_STANDARD_MODE_POSITION_HOLD =1 , MAV_STANDARD_MODE_ORBIT =2 , MAV_STANDARD_MODE_CRUISE =3 ,
  MAV_STANDARD_MODE_ALTITUDE_HOLD =4 , MAV_STANDARD_MODE_SAFE_RECOVERY =5 , MAV_STANDARD_MODE_MISSION =6 , MAV_STANDARD_MODE_LAND =7 ,
  MAV_STANDARD_MODE_TAKEOFF =8 , MAV_STANDARD_MODE_ENUM_END =9
}
 
enum  MAV_MODE_PROPERTY { MAV_MODE_PROPERTY_ADVANCED =1 , MAV_MODE_PROPERTY_NOT_USER_SELECTABLE =2 , MAV_MODE_PROPERTY_AUTO_MODE =4 , MAV_MODE_PROPERTY_ENUM_END =5 }

enum  COMPUTER_STATUS_FLAGS {
  COMPUTER_STATUS_FLAGS_UNDER_VOLTAGE =1 , COMPUTER_STATUS_FLAGS_CPU_THROTTLE =2 , COMPUTER_STATUS_FLAGS_THERMAL_THROTTLE =4 , COMPUTER_STATUS_FLAGS_DISK_FULL =8 ,
  COMPUTER_STATUS_FLAGS_ENUM_END =9
}
 
enum  AIRSPEED_SENSOR_FLAGS { AIRSPEED_SENSOR_UNHEALTHY =1 , AIRSPEED_SENSOR_USING =2 , AIRSPEED_SENSOR_FLAGS_ENUM_END =3 }
 
enum  GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC {
  GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC_UNKNOWN =0 , GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC_GNSS =1 , GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC_VISION =2 , GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC_PSEUDOLITES =3 ,
  GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC_TERRAIN =4 , GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC_MAGNETIC =5 , GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC_ESTIMATOR =6 , GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC_ENUM_END =7
}
 
enum  GLOBAL_POSITION_FLAGS { GLOBAL_POSITION_UNHEALTHY =1 , GLOBAL_POSITION_PRIMARY =2 , GLOBAL_POSITION_FLAGS_ENUM_END =3 }
 

Detailed Description

MAVLink comm protocol generated from common.xml.

See also
http://mavlink.org

Macro Definition Documentation

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION

#define HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION

Actuator configuration, used to change a setting on an actuator. Component information metadata can be used to know which outputs support which commands.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION

#define HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION

Actuator output function. Values greater or equal to 1000 are autopilot-specific.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ADSB_ALTITUDE_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_ADSB_ALTITUDE_TYPE

Enumeration of the ADSB altimeter types.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ADSB_EMITTER_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_ADSB_EMITTER_TYPE

ADSB classification for the type of vehicle emitting the transponder signal.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ADSB_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_ADSB_FLAGS

These flags indicate status such as data validity of each data source. Set = data valid.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_AIRSPEED_SENSOR_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_AIRSPEED_SENSOR_FLAGS

Airspeed sensor flags.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_AIS_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_AIS_FLAGS

These flags are used in the AIS_VESSEL.fields bitmask to indicate validity of data in the other message fields. When set, the data is valid.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_AIS_NAV_STATUS

#define HAVE_ENUM_AIS_NAV_STATUS

Navigational status of AIS vessel, enum duplicated from AIS standard, https://gpsd.gitlab.io/gpsd/AIVDM.html.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_AIS_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_AIS_TYPE

Type of AIS vessel, enum duplicated from AIS standard, https://gpsd.gitlab.io/gpsd/AIVDM.html.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ATTITUDE_TARGET_TYPEMASK

#define HAVE_ENUM_ATTITUDE_TARGET_TYPEMASK

Bitmap to indicate which dimensions should be ignored by the vehicle: a value of 0b00000000 indicates that none of the setpoint dimensions should be ignored.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_AUTOTUNE_AXIS

#define HAVE_ENUM_AUTOTUNE_AXIS

Axes that will be autotuned by MAV_CMD_DO_AUTOTUNE_ENABLE. Note that at least one flag must be set in MAV_CMD_DO_AUTOTUNE_ENABLE.param2: if none are set, the flight stack will tune its default set of axes.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_CAP_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_CAP_FLAGS

Camera capability flags (Bitmap)

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_MODE

#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_MODE

Camera Modes.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_SOURCE

#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_SOURCE

Camera sources for MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_SOURCE.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_TRACKING_MODE

#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_TRACKING_MODE

Camera tracking modes.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_TRACKING_STATUS_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_TRACKING_STATUS_FLAGS

Camera tracking status flags.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_TRACKING_TARGET_DATA

#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_TRACKING_TARGET_DATA

Camera tracking target data (shows where tracked target is within image)

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_ZOOM_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_CAMERA_ZOOM_TYPE

Zoom types for MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_ZOOM.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CAN_FILTER_OP

#define HAVE_ENUM_CAN_FILTER_OP


◆ HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE

#define HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE

Possible responses from a CELLULAR_CONFIG message.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_NETWORK_FAILED_REASON

#define HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_NETWORK_FAILED_REASON

These flags are used to diagnose the failure state of CELLULAR_STATUS.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_NETWORK_RADIO_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_NETWORK_RADIO_TYPE

Cellular network radio type.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_STATUS_FLAG

#define HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_STATUS_FLAG

These flags encode the cellular network status.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_COMP_METADATA_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_COMP_METADATA_TYPE

Supported component metadata types. These are used in the "general" metadata file returned by COMPONENT_METADATA to provide information about supported metadata types. The types are not used directly in MAVLink messages.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_COMPUTER_STATUS_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_COMPUTER_STATUS_FLAGS

Flags used to report computer status.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ENGINE_CONTROL_OPTIONS

#define HAVE_ENUM_ENGINE_CONTROL_OPTIONS

Engine control options.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE

Indicates the ESC connection type.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS

Flags to report ESC failures.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ESTIMATOR_STATUS_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_ESTIMATOR_STATUS_FLAGS

Flags in ESTIMATOR_STATUS message.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_FAILURE_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_FAILURE_TYPE

List of possible failure type to inject.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_FAILURE_UNIT

#define HAVE_ENUM_FAILURE_UNIT

List of possible units where failures can be injected.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_BREACH

#define HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_BREACH


◆ HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_MITIGATE

#define HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_MITIGATE

Actions being taken to mitigate/prevent fence breach.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_TYPE

Fence types to enable or disable when using MAV_CMD_DO_FENCE_ENABLE. Note that at least one of these flags must be set in MAV_CMD_DO_FENCE_ENABLE.param2. If none are set, the flight stack will ignore the field and enable/disable its default set of fences (usually all of them).

◆ HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS

Gimbal device (low level) capability flags (bitmap).

◆ HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS

Gimbal device (low level) error flags (bitmap, 0 means no error)

◆ HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS

Flags for gimbal device (lower level) operation.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS

Gimbal manager high level capability flags (bitmap). The flags are identical to the GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS. However, the gimbal manager does not need to copy the flags from the gimbal but can also enhance the capabilities and thus add flags.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS

Flags for high level gimbal manager operation The first 16 bits are identical to the GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_GLOBAL_POSITION_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_GLOBAL_POSITION_FLAGS

Status flags for GLOBAL_POSITION.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC

#define HAVE_ENUM_GLOBAL_POSITION_SRC

Source for GLOBAL_POSITION measurement or estimate.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_GPS_FIX_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_GPS_FIX_TYPE

Type of GPS fix.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_GPS_INPUT_IGNORE_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_GPS_INPUT_IGNORE_FLAGS


◆ HAVE_ENUM_GRIPPER_ACTIONS

#define HAVE_ENUM_GRIPPER_ACTIONS

Gripper actions.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_HIGHRES_IMU_UPDATED_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_HIGHRES_IMU_UPDATED_FLAGS

Flags in the HIGHRES_IMU message indicate which fields have updated since the last message.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS_FLAGS

Flags used in HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS message.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_HIL_SENSOR_UPDATED_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_HIL_SENSOR_UPDATED_FLAGS

Flags in the HIL_SENSOR message indicate which fields have updated since the last message.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_HL_FAILURE_FLAG

#define HAVE_ENUM_HL_FAILURE_FLAG

Flags to report failure cases over the high latency telemetry.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ILLUMINATOR_ERROR_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_ILLUMINATOR_ERROR_FLAGS

Illuminator module error flags (bitmap, 0 means no error)

◆ HAVE_ENUM_ILLUMINATOR_MODE

#define HAVE_ENUM_ILLUMINATOR_MODE

Modes of illuminator.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_LANDING_TARGET_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_LANDING_TARGET_TYPE

Type of landing target.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAG_CAL_STATUS

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAG_CAL_STATUS


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ARM_AUTH_DENIED_REASON

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ARM_AUTH_DENIED_REASON

Reasons for denying an authorization request made with MAV_CMD_ARM_AUTHORIZATION_REQUEST. If the COMMAND_ACK result is MAV_RESULT_DENIED, this is used to set the reason in the result_param2 field.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE

Enumeration for battery charge states.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FAULT

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FAULT

Smart battery supply status/fault flags (bitmask) for health indication. The battery must also report either MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_FAILED or MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_UNHEALTHY if any of these are set.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION

Enumeration of battery functions.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_MODE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_MODE

Battery mode. Note, the normal operation mode (i.e. when flying) should be reported as MAV_BATTERY_MODE_UNKNOWN to allow message trimming in normal flight.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_TYPE

Enumeration of battery types.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_CMD

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_CMD

Commands to be executed by the MAV. They can be executed on user request, or as part of a mission script. If the action is used in a mission, the parameter mapping to the waypoint/mission message is as follows: Param 1, Param 2, Param 3, Param 4, X: Param 5, Y:Param 6, Z:Param 7. This command list is similar what ARINC 424 is for commercial aircraft: A data format how to interpret waypoint/mission data. NaN and INT32_MAX may be used in float/integer params (respectively) to indicate optional/default values (e.g. to use the component's current yaw or latitude rather than a specific value). See https://mavlink.io/en/guide/xml_schema.html#MAV_CMD for information about the structure of the MAV_CMD entries.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_COLLISION_ACTION

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_COLLISION_ACTION

Possible actions an aircraft can take to avoid a collision.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_COLLISION_SRC

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_COLLISION_SRC

Source of information about this collision.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_COLLISION_THREAT_LEVEL

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_COLLISION_THREAT_LEVEL

Aircraft-rated danger from this threat.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DATA_STREAM

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DATA_STREAM

A data stream is not a fixed set of messages, but rather a recommendation to the autopilot software. Individual autopilots may or may not obey the recommended messages.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR

Enumeration of distance sensor types.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DO_REPOSITION_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DO_REPOSITION_FLAGS

Bitmap of options for the MAV_CMD_DO_REPOSITION.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE

Enumeration of estimator types.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_EVENT_CURRENT_SEQUENCE_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_EVENT_CURRENT_SEQUENCE_FLAGS

Flags for CURRENT_EVENT_SEQUENCE.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_EVENT_ERROR_REASON

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_EVENT_ERROR_REASON

Reason for an event error response.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FRAME

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FRAME

Coordinate frames used by MAVLink. Not all frames are supported by all commands, messages, or vehicles.

Global frames use the following naming conventions:

  • "GLOBAL": Global coordinate frame with WGS84 latitude/longitude and altitude positive over mean sea level (MSL) by default. The following modifiers may be used with "GLOBAL":
    • "RELATIVE_ALT": Altitude is relative to the vehicle home position rather than MSL.
    • "TERRAIN_ALT": Altitude is relative to ground level rather than MSL.
    • "INT": Latitude/longitude (in degrees) are scaled by multiplying by 1E7.

Local frames use the following naming conventions:

  • "LOCAL": Origin of local frame is fixed relative to earth. Unless otherwise specified this origin is the origin of the vehicle position-estimator ("EKF").
  • "BODY": Origin of local frame travels with the vehicle. NOTE, "BODY" does NOT indicate alignment of frame axis with vehicle attitude.
  • "OFFSET": Deprecated synonym for "BODY" (origin travels with the vehicle). Not to be used for new frames.

Some deprecated frames do not follow these conventions (e.g. MAV_FRAME_BODY_NED and MAV_FRAME_BODY_OFFSET_NED).

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FTP_ERR

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FTP_ERR

MAV FTP error codes (may be used in FILE_TRANSFER_PROTOCOL). See https://mavlink.io/en/services/ftp.html.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FTP_OPCODE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FTP_OPCODE

MAV FTP opcodes (may be used in FILE_TRANSFER_PROTOCOL). See https://mavlink.io/en/services/ftp.html.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FUEL_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FUEL_TYPE

Fuel types for use in FUEL_TYPE. Fuel types specify the units for the maximum, available and consumed fuel, and for the flow rates.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG

Flags to report status/failure cases for a power generator (used in GENERATOR_STATUS). Note that FAULTS are conditions that cause the generator to fail. Warnings are conditions that require attention before the next use (they indicate the system is not operating properly).

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GOTO

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GOTO

Actions that may be specified in MAV_CMD_OVERRIDE_GOTO to override mission execution.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_LANDED_STATE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_LANDED_STATE

Enumeration of landed detector states.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_RESULT

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_RESULT

Result of mission operation (in a MISSION_ACK message).

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_TYPE

Type of mission items being requested/sent in mission protocol.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MODE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MODE

Predefined OR-combined MAV_MODE_FLAG values. These can simplify using the flags when setting modes. Note that manual input is enabled in all modes as a safety override.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MODE_PROPERTY

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MODE_PROPERTY

Mode properties.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MOUNT_MODE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MOUNT_MODE

Enumeration of possible mount operation modes. This message is used by obsolete/deprecated gimbal messages.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_ARM_STATUS

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_ARM_STATUS


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_AUTH_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_CLASSIFICATION_TYPE


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_DESC_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_DESC_TYPE


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_HEIGHT_REF

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_HEIGHT_REF


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_HOR_ACC

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_HOR_ACC


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_ID_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_ID_TYPE


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_OPERATOR_ID_TYPE

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_OPERATOR_LOCATION_TYPE

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_SPEED_ACC

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_SPEED_ACC


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_STATUS

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_STATUS


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_TIME_ACC

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_TIME_ACC


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_UA_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_UA_TYPE


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_VER_ACC

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ODID_VER_ACC


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_ERROR

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_ERROR

Parameter protocol error types (see PARAM_ERROR).

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE

Specifies the datatype of a MAVLink extended parameter.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_TYPE

Specifies the datatype of a MAVLink parameter.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_POWER_STATUS

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_POWER_STATUS

Power supply status flags (bitmask)

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_RESULT

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_RESULT

Result from a MAVLink command (MAV_CMD)

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ROI

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ROI

The ROI (region of interest) for the vehicle. This can be be used by the vehicle for camera/vehicle attitude alignment (see MAV_CMD_NAV_ROI).

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION

Enumeration of sensor orientation, according to its rotations.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SEVERITY

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SEVERITY

Indicates the severity level, generally used for status messages to indicate their relative urgency. Based on RFC-5424 using expanded definitions at: http://www.kiwisyslog.com/kb/info:-syslog-message-levels/.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_STANDARD_MODE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_STANDARD_MODE

Standard modes with a well understood meaning across flight stacks and vehicle types. For example, most flight stack have the concept of a "return" or "RTL" mode that takes a vehicle to safety, even though the precise mechanics of this mode may differ. The modes supported by a flight stack can be queried using AVAILABLE_MODES and set using MAV_CMD_DO_SET_STANDARD_MODE. The current mode is streamed in CURRENT_MODE. See https://mavlink.io/en/services/standard_modes.html.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR

These encode the sensors whose status is sent as part of the SYS_STATUS message.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED

These encode the sensors whose status is sent as part of the SYS_STATUS message in the extended fields.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_TUNNEL_PAYLOAD_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_TUNNEL_PAYLOAD_TYPE


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_VTOL_STATE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_VTOL_STATE

Enumeration of VTOL states.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAV_WINCH_STATUS_FLAG

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_WINCH_STATUS_FLAG

Winch status flags used in WINCH_STATUS.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE


◆ HAVE_ENUM_MISSION_STATE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MISSION_STATE

States of the mission state machine. Note that these states are independent of whether the mission is in a mode that can execute mission items or not (is suspended). They may not all be relevant on all vehicles.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MOTOR_TEST_ORDER

#define HAVE_ENUM_MOTOR_TEST_ORDER

Sequence that motors are tested when using MAV_CMD_DO_MOTOR_TEST.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_MOTOR_TEST_THROTTLE_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_MOTOR_TEST_THROTTLE_TYPE

Defines how throttle value is represented in MAV_CMD_DO_MOTOR_TEST.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS

#define HAVE_ENUM_NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS


◆ HAVE_ENUM_NAV_VTOL_LAND_OPTIONS

#define HAVE_ENUM_NAV_VTOL_LAND_OPTIONS


◆ HAVE_ENUM_ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR

#define HAVE_ENUM_ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR

Yaw behaviour during orbit flight.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_PARACHUTE_ACTION

#define HAVE_ENUM_PARACHUTE_ACTION

Parachute actions. Trigger release and enable/disable auto-release.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_PARAM_ACK

#define HAVE_ENUM_PARAM_ACK

Result from PARAM_EXT_SET message.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_POSITION_TARGET_TYPEMASK

#define HAVE_ENUM_POSITION_TARGET_TYPEMASK

Bitmap to indicate which dimensions should be ignored by the vehicle: a value of 0b0000000000000000 or 0b0000001000000000 indicates that none of the setpoint dimensions should be ignored. If bit 9 is set the floats afx afy afz should be interpreted as force instead of acceleration.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_PRECISION_LAND_MODE

#define HAVE_ENUM_PRECISION_LAND_MODE

Precision land modes (used in MAV_CMD_NAV_LAND).

◆ HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER

#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER

Action for the accelerometer (param5) of MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER

#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER

Action for the magnetometer (param2) of MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION

#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION

Actions for reading and writing plan information (mission, rally points, geofence) between persistent and volatile storage when using MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE. (Commonly missions are loaded from persistent storage (flash/EEPROM) into volatile storage (RAM) on startup and written back when they are changed.)

◆ HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION

#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION

Actions for reading/writing parameters between persistent and volatile storage when using MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE. (Commonly parameters are loaded from persistent storage (flash/EEPROM) into volatile storage (RAM) on startup and written back when they are changed.)

◆ HAVE_ENUM_RC_SUB_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_RC_SUB_TYPE

RC sub-type of types defined in RC_TYPE. Used in MAV_CMD_START_RX_PAIR. Ignored if value does not correspond to the set RC_TYPE.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_RC_TYPE

#define HAVE_ENUM_RC_TYPE

RC type. Used in MAV_CMD_START_RX_PAIR.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION

#define HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION

Reboot/shutdown action for selected component in MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS

#define HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS

Specifies the conditions under which the MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN command should be accepted.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_RTK_BASELINE_COORDINATE_SYSTEM

#define HAVE_ENUM_RTK_BASELINE_COORDINATE_SYSTEM

RTK GPS baseline coordinate system, used for RTK corrections.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_SAFETY_SWITCH_STATE

#define HAVE_ENUM_SAFETY_SWITCH_STATE

Possible safety switch states.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV

SERIAL_CONTROL device types.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG

#define HAVE_ENUM_SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG

SERIAL_CONTROL flags (bitmask)

◆ HAVE_ENUM_SET_FOCUS_TYPE

Focus types for MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_FOCUS.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_SPEED_TYPE

Speed setpoint types used in MAV_CMD_DO_CHANGE_SPEED.

Flags to indicate the status of camera storage.

◆ HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_TYPE

Flags to indicate the type of storage.

Tune formats (used for vehicle buzzer/tone generation).

Video stream encodings.

Stream status flags (Bitmap)
